data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "No single fixed (p, r) regime suffices; harness strictly beats SC only when critic operating point is question-adaptive: critic confidence c(q) is high on questions with fragile majorities (margin <= 1) and low on questions with robust majorities, so that filtering concentrates TPs where they matter and avoids FPs where they don't.", "mechanism": "Critics with fixed thresholds face an inevitable precision-recall tradeoff: any threshold aggressive enough to catch marginal-bad candidates also flags marginal-good candidates. On robust-majority questions, these FPs are pure loss (SC already correct, filtering can only break). On fragile-majority questions, TPs are high-value (can flip a 3-2 wrong to 2-2 tie or 3-2 right to 2-3). An adaptive critic that raises its threshold on robust-majority questions and lowers it on fragile ones shifts operating point per-question, achieving p(q), r(q) that clear p_min on fragile questions while avoiding FPs on robust ones. Repository nulls are explained: the critic operated at a fixed threshold, and all questions were either ceiling (robust majority) or too easy, so the adaptive mechanism never engaged.", "scope": {"covers": ["k>=5 any parity", "question-adaptive critic threshold c(q)", "mixed-difficulty item sets where some questions have fragile majorities", "critic with access to candidate-similarity or margin signals"], "excludes": ["fixed-threshold critics (cannot escape the precision-recall tradeoff across all question types)", "uniform-difficulty sets where all questions have the same majority margin", "critic without any signal to adapt on (no margin, no similarity, no confidence)"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Adaptive-critic harness accuracy <= fixed-threshold harness accuracy on a set containing >= 30% fragile-majority questions", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Critic confidence c(q) shows no correlation with majority-margin (|rho| < 0.15) yet adaptive harness beats SC", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "FP rate on robust-majority questions under adaptive threshold exceeds FP rate under fixed threshold", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "Directly extends 30e483eb and negates 559750's base-case. The decisive experiment must induce fragile majorities AND give the critic a margin/similarity signal to adapt on, then compare adaptive vs fixed-threshold filtering. Budget allocation: ~120k tokens for hard-set generation (difficult items targeting e~0.3-0.5 at k=5), ~30k for candidate pool generation, ~30k for adaptive critic (prompted with margin signal), ~30k for fixed-threshold critic, ~90k for evaluation across 3 arms."}
